name: HWR_FLW_OPERATION_B
description: The operation table is used to store operations.

columns.Name: OPERATION_ID
columns.Comments: This is the primary key of the operation table.

columns.Name: UUID
columns.Comments: This is the generated instance identifier associated to this operation.

columns.Name: REQUEST_OD
columns.Comments: The request object definition of the operation.

columns.Name: RESPONSE_OD
columns.Comments: The response object definition of the Operation.

columns.Name: NAME
columns.Comments: The display name value for the operation.

columns.Name: OPERATION_TYPE
columns.Comments: This stores the type of Operation.

columns.Name: CONNECTION_SPEC
columns.Comments: The connection spec for the operation.

columns.Name: IS_SEEDED_DATA
columns.Comments: A flag to indicate the whether the operation is seeded: Y for yes; N for no.

columns.Name: IS_INTERNAL
columns.Comments: A flag to indicate the internal state: Y for yes; N for no.

columns.Name: CREATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who created the row.

columns.Name: CREATION_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the creation of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who last updated the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the last update of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_LOGIN
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the session login associated to the user who last updated the row.

columns.Name: OBJECT_VERSION_NUMBER
columns.Comments: Used to implement optimistic locking. This number is incremented every time that the row is updated. The number is compared at the start and end of a transaction to detect whether another session has updated the row since it was queried.
